headline:
US brain,
Chinese body
deck: Reference designs reveal American AI software operating on Chinese robot bodies while export rules govern their combination.
body: Engineers at Nvidia have paired the GR00T humanoid software stack with a physical platform built by Unitree, creating a working reference that underscores the trans-Pacific division of labor in advanced robotics.
Unitree has accelerated preparations for a Shanghai listing, positioning the company to raise capital amid rising global demand for cost-effective humanoid hardware.

Production teams at Tesla continue converting assembly lines in support of the Optimus program, yet the decisive factors remain supply-chain access and regulatory navigation rather than demonstration videos alone.
US export controls targeting advanced semiconductors and AI systems have increased compliance burdens on any integration of American software leadership with Chinese mechanical production.
The humanoid sector at present operates as a contest of engineering timelines and manufacturing cost curves, not yet as a deployment race measured in operational robot numbers.

This configuration therefore sets US artificial intelligence capabilities onto Chinese-built robot bodies, an arrangement that export restrictions have turned into an immediate policy matter for authorities on both sides.[E1]

evidence_box.fragment: the global race for physical AI
